
---
title: "Econ 396 HW3 guide"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-24"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```


Question 1


### check that link works
```{r}

ecolidata <- read.csv("https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/ecoli/ecoli.data", header = FALSE)
```
```



```{r}
irisdata <- read.csv("https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/iris/iris.data", header = FALSE)
```


Name the columns

```{r}

#rename columns based on metadata

names(irisdata) <- c("sepalL","sepalW","petalL","petalw","class")
```

7. Attribute Information:
   1. sepal length in cm
   2. sepal width in cm
   3. petal length in cm
   4. petal width in cm
   5. class: 
      -- Iris Setosa
      -- Iris Versicolour
      -- Iris Virginica
```{r}
### summarizes everything and gives the correct data type

irisdata$class <- as.factor(irisdata$class)

str(irisdata)
summary(irisdata)
head(irisdata)

```


```{r}
summary(irisdata$class)

versidata <- subset(irisdata, irisdata$class == "Iris-versicolor")

summary(versidata)
summary(othersubset)
```


```{r}
othersubset <- subset(irisdata, irisdata$class == "Iris-setosa" | irisdata$class == "Iris-virginica")

```


Answer the question

Hmm it seems like the type of plant influences it's petal length.... viriginica might have the smallest petals..




Question 2

```{r}
### retrieve data
cardata <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\ottenheimew\\Documents\\Econ396-Rep\\Homework Data\\car.csv")
```


```{r}
head(cardata)

glimpse(cardata)
view(cardata)
```



```{r}
#print(cardata)
```

### Question 3 {.tabset}




```{r}

examplefunction <- function(flowerstat1, flowerstat2) {
  lengthtotal <- flowerstat1 + flowerstat2
  return(lengthtotal)
}


irisdata$Lengthsum <- examplefunction(irisdata$sepalL,irisdata$petalL)
```

(𝑦 ― 𝑄3) > 1.5 ∗ 𝐼𝑄𝑅 OR (𝑄1 ― 𝑦) > 1.5 ∗ 𝐼𝑄𝑅

```{r}
### IMPORTANT THIS FUNCTION NEEDS TIDYVERSE
###library(tidyverse)
### Trim function
### Remove data that is above or below the 1.5 * IQR

#print(quantile(irisdata$sepalW))
#print(IQR(irisdata$sepalW))

irisdata$SWQ1 <- quantile(irisdata$sepalW,probs =  .25)

trim.CV <- function(dataset,flower) {
  ### this finds the Q 1
  Q1 <- quantile(flower, probs = .25)
  ### this finds the Q 3
  Q3 <- quantile(flower, probs = .75)
  ### computes IQR
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  
  ### classifies data as outliers if they exceed threshold of IQR
  Outlier <- if_else((flower - Q3) > 1.5*IQR | (Q1 - flower)> 1.5*IQR,1,0 )
  ### assigns outlier identifier to data set
  dataset$Outlier <- Outlier
  ### subsets data if not an outlier
  Trimmeddata <- subset(dataset,dataset$Outlier == 0)
  
  ### return subset data
  return(Trimmeddata)
  
}

IrisNoOuts <- trim.CV(irisdata,irisdata$sepalW)


```

```{r}

print(sapply(irisdata, FUN = mean))

listofmeans <- lapply(irisdata, FUN = mean)
```
